name: Derryfield Country Club
about: Derryfield Country Club is an eighteen-hole regulation public golf course located in Manchester, NH, established in 1932. It is one of only three municipally-owned golf courses in New Hampshire. The facility features both outdoor golf and indoor PGA Tour Simulators, along with a full-service restaurant and pro shop. The venue offers golf instruction, leagues, tournaments, and season permits for regular players. Its municipal ownership positions it as a community-accessible option for both casual play and serious practice.
why_golfers_like_it: Combination of outdoor course play and indoor PGA Tour Simulators for year-round practice
Municipal ownership provides accessible pricing and community-oriented atmosphere
Full-service restaurant and pro shop enhance the overall experience
Variety of leagues and tournaments for regular and competitive play

food_and_drink.notes: Derryfield Restaurant & Lounge serves full service meals and a snack shack is also available
Full service bar available at Derryfield Restaurant & Lounge

simulator_hardware.notes: Three indoor golf simulators available at the facility

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: You can bring your own clubs or rent equipment from the pro shop, which offers club rentals.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drink policies are not specified, but the venue offers a full-service restaurant and snack shack for on-site dining and beverages.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide specific timing for 18 holes, but tee times are required 7 days per week and can be scheduled online or by phone.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Derryfield Country Club offers a PGA Junior League and provides a family-friendly environment with both indoor and outdoor activities suitable for children.
